hadiths.chapter: 127. CHAPTER ON THE HUSBAND’S RIGHTS OVER HIS
hadiths.ar:
4524  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ز َّوْج ِهَّا مَّا رَّإ َّي ْث ُّ ق َّظ ُّ  
مِن ْ وَّج ْهِك َّ ج َّي ْزإً ق َّق َّذْ جَّن ِظَّ عَّمَّلُّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: 
"Any woman who removes her clothing outside her husband's house or without his 
permission remains under the curse of Allah {SWT} until she returns to her home." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.440 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.215 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 
p.778 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 127, H. 4523

hadiths.chapter: 127. CHAPTER ON THE HUSBAND’S RIGHTS OVER HIS
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Abu Abdillah {a.s} that he said: 
"Any woman who says to her husband, 'I have never seen any good from you,' then her 
deeds are rendered void." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.440 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.781 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.20 
p.162

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 127, H. 4524

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.en:
Al-Alaa ibn Razin narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Jafar Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} that the Messenger of Allah {SWT} said: 
"Archangel Jibril {a.s} advised me regarding women so much that I thought divorce 
should not be permissible except in the case of clear indecency."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, V.5 p.512 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V.3 p.440 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, V.1 p.216 • 
Uddat Al-Da’i, V.1 p.91 • Al-Wafi, V.22 p.786 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, V.20 p.170 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.253 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4525
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.en:
Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Imam Abu Abdillah {a.s} about the right of a woman 
over her husband. 
Imam {a.s} said: "He should satisfy her hunger, clothe her body, and if she acts 
ignorantly, he should forgive her." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.440 • Tafsir Al-Safi, Vol.1 p.257 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4526

reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar: 4528 - وَّ ق َّالَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم كَّان َّ ي َّع ْق ِزُّ لَّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Ibrahim {a.s}, the Friend of the Most Merciful {SWT}, complained to Allah {SWT},  
about the behavior of Sarah {s.a}. So Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him: 
"The example of a woman is like the example of a rib, if you try to straighten it, it will 
break, and if you leave it as it is, you will benefit from it."  
I (narrator) asked: “Who said this?"  
Imam {a.s} became angry and said: "By Allah {SWT}, it is the saying of Prophet of Allah {SWT}." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.440 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4527

reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٢٧

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4529  - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّاصِمُّ ن ْن ُّ جُّمَّي ْذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّصِي زٍ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْث ُّ إ َّن َّا ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ي َّق ُّول 
ق َّلَّمْ ي َّكْسُّهَّا مَّا ي ُّوَّإرِى  عَّوْرَّي َّهَّا وَّ ي ُّطْعِمْهَّا مَّا ي ُّق ِي مُّ صُّلْن َّهَّا كَّان َّ جَّق  اً عَّلَّى إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّق َّزِّق َّ ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
Abu Abdillah {a.s} said: 
"My father {a.s} had a wife who used to harm him, yet he would forgive her." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.441 • Awalim Al-Uloom, Vol.19 p.345 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4528
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٢٨

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4530 - وَّ رَّوَّى رِن ْعِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّسَّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  ق وْلِهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ:   
۝  وَّ مَّن ْ ق ُّذِرَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ رِر ْق ُّهُّ ق َّلْي ُّن ْق ِق ْ مِمّٰا إ ي ٰاةُّ إَّللّٰهُّ  ۝  ق َّال 
ن َّي ْن َّهُّمَّا
hadiths.en:
Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Abu Basir, who said: I heard Abu Jafar Imam 
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} say: 
"Whoever has a wife and does not provide her with clothing to cover her modesty and 
food to sustain her strength, it is the duty of the Imam to separate them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.441 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.217 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 
p.789 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.21 p.509 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4529
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٢٩

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4531  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو إَّلص َّن  َّاحِ إَّلْكِن َّان ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال   ج َّمْسَّهَّا وَّ صَّامَّث ْ س َّهْزَّهَّا 
وَّ جَّح  َّث ْ ن َّي ْث َّ رَّن ِّهَّا وَّ إ َّطَّاعَّث ْ ر َّوْج َّهَّا وَّ عَّزَّق َّث ْ جَّق  َّ عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّلْي َّذْج ُّلْ مِن ْ إ َّى ِّ إ َّن ْوَّإ ب ِ إَّلْح ِن َّان ِ س َّاءَّب ْ
hadiths.en:
Rubi ibn Abdullah and Al -Fudhayl ibn Yasar narrated from Abu Abdillah {a.s} 
regarding the saying of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic:  
"And whoever has his provision restricted, let him spend from what Allah {SWT} has given 
him" (Surah At-Talaq 65:7). 
Imam {a.s} said: "If he provides for her what sustains her strength along with clothing, 
then it is sufficient. Otherwise, they shall be separated."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.441 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.217 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.21 p.509 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.5 p.412 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4530
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٣٠

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4532  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ عَّن ْ عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ِ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال « :مِن 
إَّلْأ َّن ْصَّارِ عَّلَّى عَّهْذِ رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ج َّزَّح َّ ق ِى  ن َّعْض ِ جَّوَّإن ِح ِهِ وَّ عَّهِذَّ إ ِلَّى إِ مْزَّإ َّي ِهِ عَّهْذإً إ َّلأ َّ ي َّح ْزُّح َّ مِن ْ 
ن َّي ْي ِهَّا جَّي  َّى ي َّق ْذَّم لَّى رَّسُّولِ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ق َّق َّالَّث ْ إ ِن  َّ ر َّوْج ِى   
ج َّزَّح َّ وَّ عَّهِذَّ إ ِلَّى  َّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ إ َّج ْزُّح َّ مِن ْ ن َّي ْي ِى  جَّي  َّى ي َّق ْذَّمَّ وَّ إ ِن  َّ إ َّن ِى  مَّزِي ض   ق َّي َّا ْمُّزُّن ِى  إ َّن ْ إ َّعُّوذَّةُّ ق َّ ق َّال 
ن َّي ْي ِك ِ وَّ إ َّطِي عِى  ر َّوْج َّ ك ِعَّلَّي ْهِ 
ق َّق َّال  صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ
hadiths.en:
Abu al-Sabbah al-Kinani narrated from Abu Abdillah {a.s} who said: 
"If a woman performs her five daily prayers, fasts during her month (Ramadan), 
performs Hajj to the House of her Lord {AZJ}, obeys her husband, and acknowledges the 
right of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s}, then she may enter Paradise through whichever gate 
she wishes." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.441 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.159 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
«

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4531

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4533 - : وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ ق َّوْلِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل ۝ ق ُّوإ إ َّن ْق ُّسَّكُّمْ وَّ إ َّهْلِي كُّمْ ن ٰارإً ۝   
كَّي ْف َّ ن َّق ِي هِن  َّ ق َّال ي َّق ْن َّلْن َّ ق َّال 
ق َّق َّذْ ق َّص َّي ْي ُّمْ مَّا عَّلَّي ْكُّمْ
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Abi Umayr narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan, from Abu 
Abdillah {a.s}, who said: 
A man from the Ansar, during the time of the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and 
blessings be upon him and his family), went out for some errands and instructed his 
wife not to leave her house until he returned. 
Her father became ill, so the woman sent a message to the Messenger of Allah {SWT} 
(peace and blessings be upon him and his family) saying: "My husband has gone out 
and instructed me not to leave my house until he returns, but my father is ill. Do you 
command me to visit him?" 
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} said: "No, stay in your house and obey your husband." 
Then her father died, so she sent another message, saying: "O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, 
my father has died. Do you command me to perform the funeral prayer for him?"  
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "No, stay in your house and 
obey your husband." 
After the man was buried, the Messenger of Allah {SWT} sent her a message saying: 
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has forgiven you and your father because 
of your obedience to your husband." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.441 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.216 • Mustadrak Al -
Wasa’il, Vol.14 p.258 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4532
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٣٢

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4534   -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن ُّ سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ ذ َّرُّوهُّن  َّ 
ن َّلْهَّاء
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the saying of Allah 
{SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic:  
"Protect yourselves and your families from a Fire" (Surah At-Tahrim, 66:6).  
How do we protect them? 
Imam {a.s} said: "You command them and forbid them." 
It was said to him: "We command them and forbid them, but they do not accept."  
Imam {a.s} said: "When you have commanded them and forbidden them, then you have 
fulfilled your responsibility." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.442 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.798 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.20 
p.177 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Vol.5 p.373 • Tafsir Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol.13 p.336 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4533
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٣٣

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4535  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  ر ِي َّاذٍ عَّن ْ ج َّعْق َّزِ ن ْن ِ مُّحَّم َّذٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ْ إ ن َّان ِهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ مُّ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ ق َّالَّ  
رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ن ِسَّاءَّكُّمُّ إَّلْع ُّزَّف َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّعَّلِّمُّوهُّن  َّ إَّلْكِي َّان َّه َّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّعَّلِّمُّوهُّن  َّ سُّورَّة َّ ي ُّوسُّف َّ وَّ  
عَّلِّمُّوهُّن  َّ إَّلْمِع ْز َّلَّ وَّ سُّورَّة َّ إَّلن  ُّورِ
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdillah {a.s} who said: 
"Inspire them with the love of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} and leave them in their natural 
simplicity." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.442 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.799 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4534
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.location: Volume 3 › CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٢٨، ح ٤٥٣٤

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.ar:
4536 - وَّ رَّوَّى ص ُّزَّي ْس  إَّلْكُّن َّاسِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال  
« إَّلْمُّسَّوِّق َّاب ِ 
إَّلْمُّسَّوِّق َّاب ُّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ ق َّق َّال حَّاج َّه ِ ق َّلاَّ ي َّز َّإلُّ ي ُّسَّوِّق ُّهُّ جَّي  َّى ي َّن ْعُّسَّ ر َّوْج ُّهَّا ق َّي َّن َّامَّ 
ق َّي ِلْك َّ لأَّ ي َّز َّإلُّ إَّلْمَّلاَّن ِكَّه ُّ ي َّلْعَّن ُّهَّا جَّي  َّى ي َّسْي َّي ْق ِظ َّ ر َّوْج ُّهَّا
hadiths.en:
Ismail ibn Abi Ziyad narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s}, from his forefathers (peace be 
upon them), who said: The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him 
and his family) said: 
"Do not house your women in upper chambers, do not teach them writing, and do not 
teach them Surah Yusuf. Instead, teach them spinning and Surah An -Nur." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.1 p.374 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.442 • Al-Wafi, 
Vol.22 p.801 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.6 p.185 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, Vol.3 p.153 • Tafsir Kanz Al -Daqaiq, Vol.6 
p.268 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4535

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.en:
Durays al-Kunasi narrated from Abu Abdillah {a.s} who said: 
A woman came to the Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and 
his family) for some need, and he said to her: "Perhaps you are one of those who delay 
(their husbands)?" 
She asked: “And who are those who delay, O Messenger of Allah {SWT}?" 
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "A woman whose husband 
calls her for some need, but she keeps delaying him until he becomes drowsy and falls 
asleep. Such a woman is continuously cursed by the angels until her husband wakes 
up." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Vol.5 p.508 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.442 • Makarim Al -Akhlaq, Vol.1 
p.217 • Awali Al-La’ali, Vol.3 p.310 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.777 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.164 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4536

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: 
"May Allah {SWT} have mercy on a servant who treats his wife well, for Allah {SWT}, the 
Mighty and Majestic, has given him authority over her and made him her guardian." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.443 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.217 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 
p.789 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.170 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4537

hadiths.chapter: 128. CHAPTER ON THE WIFE’S RIGHTS OVER HER
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
said: "The best of you is the one who is best to his wives, and I am the best of you to 
my wives." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.443 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.789 • Wasa’il Al -Shi’ah, Vol.20 
p.171

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 128, H. 4538

hadiths.chapter: 129. CHAPTER ON COITUS INTERRUPTUS (
hadiths.en:
Al-Qasim ibn Yahya narrated from his grandfather Al-Hasan ibn Rashid, from 
Yaqub al-Jufi, who said: I heard Abu al-Hasan {a.s} say: 
"There is no harm in practicing withdrawal (coitus interruptus) in six cases; the woman 
who is certain that she cannot conceive, the elderly woman, the ill-mannered woman, 
the obscene woman, the woman who does not nurse her child, and the bondwoman." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.443 • Al-Khisal, Vol.1 p.328 • Uyun Al-Akhbar, Vol.1 p.278 
• Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol.7 p.491 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.755 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.152 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol.101 p.61

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 129, H. 4539

hadiths.chapter: 130. CHAPTER ON PROTECTIVE JEALOUSY
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {SWT} (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) 
said: "My father Ibrahim {a.s} was protective, and I am more protective than him. May 
Allah {SWT} humble the one among the believers who is not protective." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol.3 p.444 • Makarim Al-Akhlaq, Vol.1 p.239 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, 
Vol.20 p.154 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol.100 p.248 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 130, H. 4540

hadiths.chapter: 130. CHAPTER ON PROTECTIVE JEALOUSY
hadiths.ar:
4542   -  وَّ ق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم »
ق ِي لَّ ي َّا رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ وَّ مَّا إَّلذ َّي  ُّوب ُّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "Indeed, protective jealousy (ghayrah) is part of faith."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 3 p.444 • Shihab Al -Akhbar, Vol. 1 p.55 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 22 
p.764 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.154 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 3, Ch. 130, H. 4541

hadiths.chapter: 130. CHAPTER ON PROTECTIVE JEALOUSY
hadiths.ar:
4543  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق ُّص َّي ْلِ عَّن ْ س ُّزَّي ْسٍ إَّلْوَّإن ِس ِى ِّ عَّن ْ ج َّان ِزٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال   ق َّالَّ لِى 
إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّن َّارَّك َّ وَّ ي َّعَّالَّى لَّمْ ي َّح ْعَّلِ إَّلْع َّي ْزَّة َّ لِلن ِّسَّاءِ وَّ إ ِن  َّمَّا ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْع َّي ْزَّة َّ لِلزِّج َّ الِ لِأ َّن  َّ إَّلل َّهَّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّذْ إ َّجَّل َّ لِلز َّج ُّلِ إ َّرْن َّغَّ 
جَّزَّإن ِزَّ وَّ مَّا مَّلَّكَّث ْ ي َّمِي ن ُّهُّ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّح ْعَّلْ لِلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ إ ِلأ َّ ر َّوْج َّهَّا وَّجْذَّةُّ ق َّا ِن ْ ن َّع َّث ْ مَّغَّ ر َّوْج ِهَّا ع َّي ْ زَّةُّ كَّان َّث ْ عِن ْذَّ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ 
ر َّإن ِي َّه ً وَّ إ ِن  َّ مَّا ي َّع َّارُّ إَّلْمُّن ْكِزَّإب ُّ مِن ْهُّن  َّ ق َّا َّم َّا إَّلْمُّو ْمِن َّاب ُّ ق َّلا
hadiths.en:
He {saws} said: "Indeed, the fragrance of Paradise can be perceived from a 
distance of five hundred years, but one who is disobedient to his parents and a 
cuckold (dayyuth) will not perceive it." 
It was asked: “O Messenger of Allah {SWT}, who is the dayyuth?" 
He (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) said: "The one whose wife 
commits adultery, and he is aware of it." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol.3 p.444 • Al-Khisal, Vol.1 p.37 • Rawdat Al -Wa’izin, Vol.2 
p.366 • Al-Wafi, Vol.22 p.764 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.154 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’ah, Vol.20 p.327 • Bihar Al-
Anwar, Vol.76 p.114 
﴿HADITH ﴾
